body:
This PR adds a field `erroring` to the `NaNChecker`, and also makes the `NaNChecker` mutable. This means that the default `NaNChecker` can be converted to an erroring `NaNChecker` by writing

```julia
simulation.callbacks[:nan_checker].func.erroring = true
```

or

```julia
erroring_NaNChecker!(simulation)
```

TODO

- [x] Test

Resolves #2086 .
title: Allow NaNChecker.erroring
